channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Myles Garrett thinks Browns are in a better place after extension, discussions about team's future
item.description: Cleveland edge rusher Myles Garrett believes a trade request and voicing his frustrations during Super Bowl week about the Browns’ 3-14 campaign was the catalyst for more conversations with general manager Andrew Berry about the franchise’s future.
item.dc:creator: Joe Reedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: NFL Draft: How to watch, dates, draft order and potential top picks
item.description: Quarterbacks Cam Ward and Shedeur Sanders figure to be among the top picks in next month’s NFL draft, though there’s no current consensus that either player will be the No. 1 overall selection.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: It's not just Jamaica with a 'Cool Runnings' story. There are sliders from all over the world now
item.description: LAKE PLACID, N.Y. (AP) — It doesn’t snow in Jamaica. Or Malta. Or Ghana. There’s been a maybe dusting reported on rare occasions in parts of Thailand and Malaysia. And nobody thinks of Spain, Colombia, Israel, Brazil and Taiwan as winter sports superpowers. They’re all sliding anyway.
item.dc:creator: TIM REYNOLDS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Stephen Curry becomes first player in NBA history to make 4,000 3-pointers
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Stephen Curry's shooting range and accuracy from way back have long stood alone — whether with a defender or two in his face or when letting it fly from the logo.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Teen track runner who bashed opponent's head rallies supporters in Virginia after getting assault charge
item.description: Virginia teen Alaila Everett held a rally outside of her high school Thursday, as supporters showed sympathy amid national backlash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Michigan's Dusty May reveals what he learned in first year coaching in Big Ten
item.description: Michigan Wolverines head coach Dusty May reveals what he learned in his first year coaching in the Big Ten during an appearance on OutKick.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Not just Ohtani: Tokyo Series will showcase the depth of Japanese talent in Major League Baseball
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — The spotlight will be on slugger Shohei Ohtani this week when the superstar returns to Japan and leads the defending World Series champion Los Angeles Dodgers against the Chicago Cubs in the first two games of the Major League Baseball season at the Tokyo Dome.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BRANDT, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bubble watch: Indiana and Xavier now feeling the heat with Texas and North Carolina closing strong
item.description: A look at the NCAA Tournament bubble picture: Missed opportunities Xavier and Indiana had chances to give their resumes a significant boost Thursday, but the Musketeers lost 89-87 to No. 25 Marquette, and Indiana fell 72-59 to No. 23 Oregon.
item.dc:creator: NOAH TRISTER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Boys Volleyball: El Cajon Valley 3, Patrick Henry 0
item.description: EL CAJON (KUSI)- Boys volleyball action on Wednesday evening with El Cajon Valley beating Patrick Henry in three to run their record to 12-2 on the season.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Boys Volleyball: Maranatha 3, Monte Vista 1
item.description: SAN DIEGO (KUSI)- Boys volleyball Maranatha went on the road to Spring Valley and beat Monte Vista in four sets.
item.dc:creator: Nick James, Fox 5

item.title: Spurs G De'Aaron Fox to miss rest of season with finger injury
item.description: SAN ANTONIO (AP) — San Antonio Spurs guard De’Aaron Fox will miss the rest of the season with a finger injury. The Spurs said Fox will have surgery on Tuesday to repair a tendon on his left hand. Fox was injured during training camp in October while he was still with the Sacramento Kings.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: John Feinstein, bestselling author and one of the country's foremost sports writers, dies at 69
item.description: John Feinstein, one of the country’s foremost sports writers and the author of numerous bestselling books, has died unexpectedly, according to his brother, Robert Feinstein.
item.dc:creator: Howard Fendrich, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: USF Women's Basketball going to NCAA Tournament
item.description: No. 3 South Florida (23-10) defeated No. 9 Rice (17-17), 69-62, in the 2025 American Women’s Basketball Championship final on Wednesday evening, March 12, at Dickies Arena in Fort Worth, Texas.
item.dc:creator: Aileen Hnatiuk, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Minnesota fires coach Ben Johnson after 4 seasons at alma mater with 56-71 record
item.description: MINNEAPOLIS (AP) — Minnesota fired coach Ben Johnson on Thursday after four years of repeatedly rebuilding rosters at his alma mater without coming close to an NCAA Tournament appearance, which has become a fleeting accomplishment for a program that has fallen behind in the rugged and expanded Big T
item.dc:creator: DAVE CAMPBELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Brignone closes gap on super-G leader Gut-Behrami in race won by Germany's Aicher
item.description: LA THUILE, Italy (AP) — Federica Brignone closed the gap to leader Lara Gut-Behrami in the World Cup super-G standings to 45 points in a race on a shortened course won by German prodigy Emma Aicher on Thursday. Earning her second career win, Aicher edged Sofia Goggia by 0.06 seconds.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tokyo, Bristol and the A's in Sacramento are among the dates to watch for the MLB season
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The major leagues are going to Tokyo and Tennessee. West Sacramento, too. There are some old friends on the schedule, and quite possibly, a chilly reception or two on the way.
item.dc:creator: JAY COHEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The number of NHL players still playing without face visors is dwindling, only a handful left
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Ryan O’Reilly has had the conversation a number of times. Family members will often call or text to tell the veteran NHL center again that it is time to put on a visor. Sticks have nicked O’Reilly’s face. Pucks and skate blades have come perilously close to his unprotected eyes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Utah moves closer to playoff berth with 3-2 victory
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - After blowing leads in their two previous meetings with the Anaheim Ducks this season, the Utah Hockey Club wasn't about to let it happen a third time.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: UVU advances to WAC Tournament semifinals
item.description: LAS VEGAS (ABC4 Sports) - The Utah Valley men's basketball team is two wins away from its first ever bid to the NCAA Tournament. Tanner Toolson poured in 16 points as the Wolverines cruised past Utah Tech in the WAC Tournament semifinals Wednesday night at the Orleans Arena in Las Vegas, 74-57.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Myles Garrett becomes highest-paid non-quarterback in NFL history
item.description: Garrett, the 2023 AP Defensive Player of the Year, had asked for a trade last month but the Browns were adamant about keeping the 29-year-old star in Cleveland.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: RSL gives up two stoppage time goals in 3-1 loss
item.description: SANDY, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - After jumping out to an early lead, Real Salt Lake allowed three straight goals, two in stoppage time, and loses in its first meeting with San Diego FC Saturday night at America First Field, 3-1.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4
